idea-plugin.name: Node Dependency Updater
idea-plugin.description:
Inspect and update outdated Node.js dependencies from a side tool window — without dropping into the terminal. Designed for projects that ship multiple packages (workspaces / monorepos) and care which version actually gets installed.

What you get

  • One card per outdated package: current → latest with release dates.
  • Per-package version picker — choose a specific version instead of always taking latest.
  • Min release age filter — hide versions younger than N days to dodge fresh-but-broken releases.
  • Ignore checkbox per package, so Update All skips the ones you've pinned.
  • Post-update command — run a shell command (e.g. yarn install && pod install) after a successful update.
  • Workspace and dep-type badges (prod / dev / peer) so you can tell at a glance what you're updating.

Supported package managers

Auto-detected from the lockfile — npm, yarn (classic & berry), pnpm, bun.

How to open it

Tools → Update Node Dependencies…, or click the refresh icon on the main toolbar, or open the Node Dependency Updater tool window on the right edge. The action only shows up when the project has a package.json at its root.

Project-scoped settings (min age, post-update command) persist in .idea/workspace.xml, so they stay local to your machine and don't get committed.

action.description: Open the Node Dependency Updater tool window.
